title: Session event rendering and structured output
purpose: The TUI needs to render four categories of session events (assistant messages, thinking, tool calls, user messages) and handle structured output with multiple schema fields. This brief is the dispatch layer that routes events to the correct renderer and handles the per-event schema system.
task:
Implement session event rendering in events/schema_render.rs. Route assistant messages through the NT-003 markdown pipeline. Route tool calls through the NT-006/NT-007 per-tool renderers via the ToolRenderer dispatch. Render thinking content with ANSI dim (collapsed by default). Render user messages with a distinct prefix. Implement the Ctrl+E toggle for thinking visibility and secondary schema fields. Handle structured assistant messages with multiple fields: primary field renders by default, secondary fields render with labeled separators when toggled. Support automatic rendering of new event types via the labeled-section pattern.

Thinking content streams live via ThinkingDelta for real-time rendering. After the turn completes, the accumulated thinking text is persisted on the AssistantMessage.thinking field in the EventStore and is available for replay (NT-010).

requirements.title: Event rendering dispatch
requirements.spec: The event renderer SHALL route each session event type to its appropriate rendering pipeline: assistant messages to markdown, tool results to per-tool renderers (using both call arguments and result output from ProviderEvent::ToolResult), user messages to prefix renderer.
acceptance: render_event(event, caps, verbosity, thinking_visible) dispatches to the correct renderer
SessionEvent::AssistantMessage → markdown renderer (NT-003)
SessionEvent::ToolResult → per-tool renderer via renderer_for (NT-006/NT-007), passing call arguments and result output as separate Values to header_line/body
SessionEvent::UserMessage → prefix renderer (R5)
Test: render_event for an AssistantMessage produces markdown-styled output

requirements.title: Thinking content rendering
requirements.spec: ThinkingDelta events SHALL render with ANSI dim attribute in the scroll region, collapsed by default. Thinking content streams live via ThinkingDelta for real-time rendering. After the turn completes, the accumulated thinking text is persisted on the AssistantMessage.thinking field in the EventStore and is available for replay.
acceptance: ThinkingDelta text renders with dim SGR attribute when thinking_visible is true
When thinking_visible is false (default), ThinkingDelta text is silently discarded — not written to scroll region
A dim prefix (e.g. 'thinking: ') distinguishes thinking content from assistant text
After the turn completes, accumulated thinking text is available via AssistantMessage.thinking in the EventStore
During EventStore replay (NT-010), thinking content is read from AssistantMessage.thinking and rendered with the same dim treatment, controlled by the thinking_visible toggle
Test: ThinkingDelta with thinking_visible=false produces no scroll region output

requirements.title: Ctrl+E toggle for thinking and secondary fields
requirements.spec: WHEN the user presses Ctrl+E, THE SYSTEM SHALL toggle thinking visibility AND secondary schema field visibility for future output.
acceptance: DisplayToggles struct holds: thinking_visible (bool, default false), secondary_fields_visible (bool, default false)
Ctrl+E flips both booleans simultaneously
Future ThinkingDelta events respect the new thinking_visible value
Future structured messages respect the new secondary_fields_visible value
A brief status message ('thinking: on, details: on' / 'thinking: off, details: off') appears momentarily
Test: toggling from default (false, false) produces (true, true)

requirements.title: Structured assistant messages with multiple schema fields
requirements.spec: WHEN an assistant message conforms to a schema with multiple fields, THE SYSTEM SHALL render the primary field by default AND render secondary fields with labeled separators when secondary_fields_visible is true.
acceptance: Primary field: the first field in the schema (or a field named 'text', 'content', 'written', or 'response') renders by default
Secondary fields: all other fields render below the primary, each preceded by a labeled separator '─── {field_name} ───'
When secondary_fields_visible is false, secondary fields are not rendered
When secondary_fields_visible is true, all fields render in schema-definition order
Test: a 2-field schema with secondary_fields_visible=false renders only the primary field

requirements.title: User message rendering
requirements.spec: User messages SHALL render with a distinct prefix separating them from assistant output.
acceptance: User messages render with a coloured '> ' prefix (e.g. blue or cyan)
Multi-line user messages have the prefix on the first line only; continuation lines are indented
User messages are visually distinct from assistant messages (which have no prefix)
Test: user message 'hello' renders with '> hello'

requirements.title: Schema-driven rendering for new event types
requirements.spec: WHEN a new event type has a schema in the EventSchemaSet, THE SYSTEM SHALL render its content using the labeled-section pattern without requiring TUI code changes.
acceptance: render_structured(value, schema, caps, secondary_visible) renders any JSON value against its schema
Object fields render as labeled sections with separators
Primary field heuristic: 'text', 'content', 'written', 'response', or first string-typed field
Non-object values render as formatted JSON
Test: a schema with fields 'summary' and 'detail' renders 'summary' as primary, 'detail' as secondary

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the event loop or ProviderEvent subscription — that is NT-011
SHALL NOT implement multi-agent tab switching or EventStore replay — that is NT-010
SHALL NOT modify the EventStore or AssistantMessage type — thinking persistence is handled by the norn runtime, not the TUI
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: Event dispatch routes to correct renderers — verified by tests
Thinking collapsed by default, visible after toggle — verified by test
Structured multi-field messages show primary only by default — verified by test
User messages render with prefix — verified by test
Schema-driven rendering handles arbitrary schemas — verified by test
